task.title: Skip directives for markdown formatting
task.description:
Repository: astral-sh/ruff
Issue #22637

Blacken docs uses HTML comments: https://github.com/adamchainz/blacken-docs/#markdown

Do we want something different? Should we support blacken docs' directives?

rating.rationale: The agent produced a well-structured design document with clear rationale for HTML comments over info strings, blacken-docs compatibility, and reasonable code. However, this is a proposal/sketch rather than a shipped contribution — no PR was opened, no real code was committed to the ruff repo, and the test output is truncated mid-assertion. The task asked whether ruff should support blacken-docs directives, and the agent jumped straight to implementation without confirming the design direction with maintainers or checking existing discussion on the issue.
